subject: TWCB weekly update - Week of September 5, 2025
deck_filename: Counter CP SOB and NPC study.pdf

content.answer:
Hi All,


Please refer to the weekly TWCB update below.


1) TWCB Budget update

[image: image.png]

* TWCB doesn't have CBWH

Budget highlights

   -

   Toplines
   -

      ADO RR 100%, ADG RR 101%, ADO% RR 102%, ADG% RR 104%
      -

      Both ADO and ADG ADO and ADO% are on track
      -

   Aug’25 P&L projected at $3.4M (RR 94%)
   -

      MP: $2.6M PC2 (RR 92%) with PC2% at 6.0%. Below target due to
      expecting lower service fee% based on latest update that Popmart
will have
      BOD on Sep 26th (high ADG contribution while getting 4% discount for FSS
      service)
      -

      Lovito Standalone P&L: 1.2K (vs target 1.2K)
      -

      SCS Standalone P&L: $6k (vs target $6k)
      -

      SLS P&L: $860K (RR 100%)


2) TW CB OKR update (MTD: 9/3/2025)

[image: image.png]

* CB EBITDA as % CB GMV, CB SCS & Lovito Shop P&L Sep MTD will be ready 12
Sep.

# TW doesn't have TTS or LZD, nor CNLS


OKR Highlights

   -

   CB EBITDA as % CB GMV: Below target driven by lower paid ads take rate
   with only 3 day data.
   -

   Video ADO/ADG as % CB: Below target in pre-campaign period, expected to
   recover by EOM.
   -

   CPO: Below target due to a significant increase in air channel vs. SPX
   collection point based on 3 days data
   -

   Same Day shipout %: SD% below target as 3-day data included Mon & Tue,
   when sellers clear weekend backlogs.


3) Key updates/ topics for approval


[For update] XDD

   -

   XDD channel enabled order coverage at 42%, SLA at 99%
   -

   XDD conversion rate push: 202 shops with XDD shop banner set up;
   conversion rate +~1% (~37% in Jul to ~38% in Aug)


[For update] Counter Taobao:

   -

   Seller #: Total of 156 active shops in the programme (37 new shops
   joining this month)
   -

   Performance (of active sellers): MTD till 9/3 ADO 700 (RR 105% & MoM
   +23%), ADG $19.5K, Live SKU count: 65.5K (MoM +14.8K from

content.question:
And how our orders will allow us to close the gap. And how soon will we
close the gap..



This program is designed based on the study that Tina sent last month,
specifically targeting *185 brands *that contribute to* ~20% of Coupang
CB’s business.*



After matching 1.5k top SKUs from these sellers, CB has a *match rate of
50%, *and *NPC of -37%* as of July.



We plan *to close the SKU gap from 50% to 75% by Dec’25, with an increase
from this month onwards.*



For the ADG gap using this same group of *items*, we are *36% of CP
currently, and we target to close the gap to 70% by the end of the year.*







This able is very hard to understand in general. Can we always show Coupang
numbers?

SKUs, ADO, ADGMV. And subsequently how our KR CB is doing vis a
vis Coupang?
And for Coupang, are we comparing with Coupang or Coupang CB?

What is the
proper way to compare.



@Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com> can you look at this yourself and
come up with a proper table to compare, it is very painful for me right now
to understand where we are at exactly. And we have been ding donging around
this for weeks already. Can get back to me by today please.



Lastly, I do not understand why we need to take so long to improve our NPC
numbers..

*Sent:* Friday, 5 September 2025 8:26 pm
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>

*Subject:* TWCB weekly update - Week of September 5, 2025



Hi All,



Please refer to the weekly TWCB update below.



*1) TWCB Budget update*

** TWCB doesn't have CBWH*

*Budget highlights *

   - *Toplines*


   - ADO RR 100%, ADG RR 101%, ADO% RR 102%, ADG% RR 104%
      - Both ADO and ADG ADO and ADO% are on track


   - *Aug’25 P&L projected at $3.4M (RR 94%)*


   - *MP: $2.6M PC2 *(RR 92%) with PC2% at *6.0%*. Below target due to
      expecting lower service fee% based on latest update that Popmart
will have
      BOD on Sep 26th (high ADG contribution while getting 4% discount for FSS
      service)
      - *Lovito Standalone P&L: *1.2K (vs target 1.2K)
      - *SCS Standalone P&L:* $6k (vs target $6k)
      - *SLS P&L: $860K *(RR 100%)



*2) TW CB OKR update (MTD: 9/3/2025)*

** CB EBITDA as % CB GMV, CB SCS & Lovito Shop P&L Sep MTD will be ready 12
Sep.*

*# **TW doesn't have TTS or LZD, nor CNLS*



*OKR Highlights *

   - *CB EBITDA as % CB GMV:* Below target driven by lower paid ads take
   rate with only 3 day data.
   - *Video ADO/ADG as % CB: *Below target in pre-campaign period, expected
   to recover by EOM.
   - *CPO: *Below target due to a significant increase in air channel vs.
   SPX collection point based on 3 days data
   - *Same Day shipout %: *SD% below target as 3-day data included Mon &
   Tue, when sellers clear weekend backlogs.



*3) Key updates/ topics for approval *



*[For update] XDD*

   - XDD channel enabled order coverage at *42%*, SLA at *99%*
   - *XDD conversion rate push: *202 shops with XDD shop banner set up;
   conversion rate +~1% (~37% in Jul to ~38% in Aug)



*[For update] Counter Taobao*:

   - *Seller #: *Total of 156 active shops in the programme (37 new shops
   joining this month)
   - *Performance (of active sellers)*: MTD till 9/3 ADO 700 (RR 105% & MoM
   +23%), ADG $19.5K, Live SKU count: 65.5K (MoM +14.8K from the 37 new shops)
   - *Update*:


   - Excluding Lever 3 ERP, SKU target at 78% RR, RMs to i) remind sellers
      of SKU migration target, or ii) conduct migration on seller behalf is
      migration authorisation is given by seller
      - WIP pitching supercharged programme for 44 high potential TB sellers

*[For update] KR new seller program*

   - *Seller # Update: *Sep target: 14 sellers; nominated sellers: 14
   sellers (+2 MoM)


   - Isntree decided not to open TW this year, to prioritise TH & VN
      - Program will be eligible for Counter Coupang brands to enroll


   - *Sep MTD Performance: *MTD till 9/3 ADO 244 (RR 57% & MoM -27%)


   - Lagging due to pre mega campaign, expected to recover
      - 9/5 free mall campaign: 8 sellers enrolled, generated more than 300
      orders as of 4pm

*[For update] Counter Coupang*

[Follow-up question from last week]

On KR, I still do not understand the picture well, I cannot tell

   - SKU gap
   - ADO gap
   - ADGMV gap



And how our orders will allow us to close the gap. And how soon will we
close the gap..



This program is designed based on the study that Tina sent last month,
specifically targeting *185 brands *that contribute to* ~20% of Coupang
CB’s business.*



After matching 1.5k top SKUs from these sellers, CB has a *match rate of
50%, *and *NPC of -37%* as of July.



We plan *to close the SKU gap from 50% to 75% by Dec’25, with an increase
from this month onwards.*



For the ADG gap using this same group of *items*, we are *36% of CP
currently, and we target to close the gap to 70% by the end of the year.*





And who should the owner be for this project?

Is it TW side, Reg CB side or
KR side?

content.answer: Coupang vs SHP platform: to show the counter Coupang status as TW market

Coupang CB vs KR CB: to show KR CB performance, to address future CB
   initiatives

Different sub categories’ SOB, which tells FB and TKB are very bad,
   probably need to align if we can counter these categories

Lastly, I do not understand why we need to take so long to improve our NPC
numbers..

version in 1 week time)

On page 3, team put the NPC data improvement plan, we are slow is because
the npc catch plan is too small, from Sep to Dec, only 9 more sellers with
684 skus in the plan.
Yuna Kwon, can see if you can help speed up the seller pitching and sku
sourcing?

content.question:
Thanks.



As discussed



#1 - @Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com> I cannot link Slide 3 with
Slide 1 and 2 right now. I do not know how the targets are being set and
how it reconciles with Slide 1. How much of the issue is assortment, how
much of it is pricing etc.



#2 - @Ning Li <ning.li@shopee.com>, can help me look into the feasibility
of selling of KR F&B/TKB into local markets (maybe can start from TW first
given the gap.)

   - This was something I was asking for a while because of user preference
   - I also see that JPCB has a decent penetration for F&B
   - But KRCB keeps telling me not doable because of some SLS
   competitiveness, which I never really understood. Maybe can help to take
   another view on what really the issue is given that Coupang CB can do it..

Hi Ian

Had discussion with Lining and Yuna in past days. Summarize what we having
and answer your questions below:

#2 - @Ning Li, can help me look into the feasibility of selling of KR
F&B/TKB into local markets (maybe can start from TW first given the gap.)

   -

   This was something I was asking for a while because of user preference
   -

   I also see that JPCB has a decent penetration for F&B
   -

   But KRCB keeps telling me not doable because of some SLS
   competitiveness, which I never really understood. Maybe can help to take
   another view on what really the issue is given that Coupang CB can do it..

Answer:

Overall context we see

   -

   For Beauty category, KRCB have -60% NPC in Beauty category vs. Coupang
   TWCB, while the assortment gap is small as we can match 82% top selling
   assortments of Coupang TWCB (contributing 20% of Coupang TWCB beauty GMV);
   And our current hypothesis for the big pricing gap is because of Coupang
   heavy subsidy that our estimated Coupang TW P&L is -27% of GMV, whereas our
   Shopee KRCB P&L is +6.0% of GMV, there are 33% difference;
   -

   For F&B and TKB category, as we can only match ~16% of Coupang TWCB top
   selling assortments, and the key reasons for assortment gaps are:
   -

      Logistic capability to ship F&B
      -

      Lack of assortment for TKB. Acquisition/sourcing heavily focused on
      beauty before

Next step:

   -

   Logistic vendor:
   -

      to study which vendor coupang is working with to ship F&B to TW, and
      whether we can work with them and for the same quotation;
      -

      meanwhile to source other F&B capable vendors and quotations
      -

   Coupang cost break-down:
   -

      to study why our price is so much higher than coupang by comparing 4
      major cost components of pricing 1) seller settlement price with their
      suppliers (COGS) 2) logistic 3) seller margin 4) Shopee and
Coupang P

content.answer:
Current CC vendor cannot clear F&B, but we did find new options and are
currently talking with two. One vendor, JetF, gave us a quotation last week
for CC cost of 3.02USD per order which is 6.5x non-F&B (pnl simulation
pending. Target ETA 24 or 26 Sept), and this vendor also handles 90% of
Coupang KR-TW's F&B CB orders. We're using the quotation and vendor info
also collect from market intel what JetF is charging Coupang, throughout
this week, to use to negotiate back when needed. One more vendor MCT still
pending their quotation
